description: Added two bullet sub-points under item 5 (Thoughts/Feelings/Intentions/Suspicions) and two bullet sub-points under item 6 (Plans and Decisions) with concrete examples of incorrect merging behavior and explicit instructions to separate thought/decision entries from action entries.

Expanded the 'If the transcript is uncertain' bullet in the Live Session Log section into a full 'Preserve uncertainty explicitly' sub-section with four concrete examples: ambiguous success (use 'appeared to'), unclear speaker identity (use description without asserting identity), perception vs reality ('believed' vs stated fact), and partial outcomes ('may have escaped' vs 'escaped').

Added one bullet to the Critical Requirements section: 'Do not over-resolve uncertainty: when the transcript is unclear about a speaker's identity, whether an outcome succeeded, or whether a perception reflects reality, write the entry with the same level of certainty (or uncertainty) the transcript carries — use hedged wording like appeared to, seemed to, believed, or reportedly rather than asserting a definite fact.'

Added one bullet to the Recaps section: 'Do not collapse multiple recap facts into a single entry. Each separate event, discovery, decision, clue, relationship, and prior development should be its own entry. If a character found a clue AND decided to pursue a lead, those are two entries, not one.'

Rewrote the Repeated Non-Information rule in What to Remove from 'Collapse pure repetition that adds no new fictional information' to 'Collapse only exact or near-exact repetition — where the same in-game fact is restated word-for-word or in trivially equivalent phrasing with no new information added. Do not collapse two related but distinct beats just because they concern the same topic.'
